name: no-bullshit-guide-to-math-and-physics
description: Ivan Savov's No bullshit guide to math and physics — a STEM education and textbook reference toolkit covering high school to first-year university mathematics and physics with concise explanations, worked examples, and problem sets for self-study.
useCase: Learn or review essential math (algebra, calculus, vectors) and physics (mechanics, electromagnetism, waves) through concise explanations and practice problems.
knownRisks: The book covers a broad range of topics at a survey level — not deep enough for advanced study
Self-study requires discipline — the material is dense and may be overwhelming without a teacher
Math notation and equations are best understood with practice; reading alone is insufficient
